summary:
The manic/mixed episode distinction is considered unstable due to significant overlap between these states and other psychiatric conditions, especially within a spectrum of mood and organic psychoses. Clinical observations suggest this differentiation lacks consistency over time, indicating it may be more reflective of symptom variability than an enduring diagnostic category. 

(End of Round)

Final Summary:  
The manic/mixed episode distinction does not hold consistently over time because symptoms frequently blend with depression and organic psychosis, showing substantial overlap across diagnoses. Clinically, this suggests the boundaries between mania and mixtures are fluid and unreliable, reflecting instability rather than fixed categories.
